sabotage.title: Sentinel UI Sabotage Test
sabotage.desc: Simulates a component-level failure to test Aegis Sentinel recovery.
sabotage.success_msg: The UI component was autonomously patched and re-injected via Vite HMR.

general.encryption_note: "NexusOS automatically encrypts all secrets before local storage. Keys never leave your machine except for direct LLM inference calls."

daemons.no_idea: No active ideas. Start the daemon to begin autonomous ideation.

personalization.instant_apply: Changes are applied instantly across all OS layers.

chat.empty: No conversations yet. Start a new chat!
chat.welcome_subtitle: Connect to LM Studio on port 1234 to start an AI-powered conversation with real-time streaming and deep reasoning.

cortex.name: Cortex AI / Nexus Chat
cortex.desc: Your primary AI communication hub. Connect to LM Studio (port 1234) for real-time streaming chat with deep reasoning, voice input (STT), text-to-speech (TTS), and markdown rendering.
cortex.tips: Use Ctrl+Enter to send. Select different models from the sidebar dropdown. Organize conversations into folders for different projects.

monitor.name: System Monitor (OmniShield)
monitor.desc: A unified protection and monitoring dashboard. Displays real-time CPU, RAM, network traffic, and the OmniShield Health Score. Includes a Protection Ledger logging all automated heals and port snipes.
monitor.tips: The Health Score updates every 10 seconds. Check the Predictive Intelligence section for AI-generated threat analysis.

explorer.name: File Explorer
explorer.desc: Browse your local file system with a tree-view sidebar and content panel. Supports navigation, file reading, and directory listing via secure IPC channels.
explorer.tips: Double-click folders to navigate. The breadcrumb bar shows your current path.

nexus_code.name: NexusCode Editor
nexus_code.desc: A full-featured code editor powered by Monaco (VS Code engine). Supports syntax highlighting for 50+ languages, file saving, and an integrated AI assistant for code review.
nexus_code.tips: Use Ctrl+S to save. The AI sidebar can refactor and explain your code.

browser.name: Nexus Browser
browser.desc: An embedded web browser for quick lookups and web access without leaving NexusOS. Navigate any URL directly from the desktop environment.
browser.tips: Type a URL in the address bar and press Enter. Supports standard web navigation (back, forward, refresh).

vault.name: Gemini Vault
vault.desc: A secure, encrypted storage manager for sensitive data like API keys, tokens, and passwords. All data is encrypted locally with AES-256 before storage.
vault.tips: Never hardcode secrets in code. Store them here and access them programmatically via the settings API.

settings.name: System Settings
settings.desc: Configure your NexusOS environment. Includes tabs for General (language), Personalization (aura colors), AI Engine (provider selection), Thermal Sentinel (temperature monitoring), and Daemons (background services).
settings.tips: Changes to the Aura color are applied instantly across all UI components.

aura.name: Neural Aura Customizer
aura.desc: Personalize the visual identity of NexusOS. Choose from 5 aura color themes (Cyan, Purple, Emerald, Rose, Amber) that dynamically change every glow, accent, and border throughout the entire OS.
aura.tips: The selected aura persists across sessions and syncs with the lock screen.

pantheon.name: Pantheon Gallery
pantheon.desc: An intelligent image gallery that scans your filesystem for images, displays them in a masonry grid, and uses AI to analyze and describe image content.
pantheon.tips: Click 'Scan' to index images from your home directory. Use the AI analyze button to get detailed descriptions.

media_engine.name: Nexus Media Engine
media_engine.desc: A media player for audio and video content. Features a library view, curated stations, and playlist management with a sleek glassmorphism interface.
media_engine.tips: Import media files from the File Explorer or drag and drop into the player.

terminal.name: Nexus Terminal
terminal.desc: A built-in command-line terminal for executing system commands, running scripts, and managing packages directly from within NexusOS.
terminal.tips: Use it for npm/pnpm commands, git operations, or any system-level task.

prime.name: Nexus Prime Engine
prime.desc: An advanced AI code generation and patching engine. Generates code patches, manages pending changes, and provides GPU status monitoring for local LLM inference.
prime.tips: Review generated patches carefully before approving. Use the reject button to discard unwanted changes.

forge.name: Nexus Forge
forge.desc: A rapid prototyping and component building tool. Create and test UI components in isolation before integrating them into the main application.
forge.tips: Perfect for experimenting with new designs without risking the main codebase.

animus.name: Animus Evolution Vault
animus.desc: An autonomous code evolution daemon that scans existing codebase components, generates improvement suggestions, and injects approved changes back into the source files.
animus.tips: Start the daemon from Settings > Daemons. Review queued items before injection.

architect.name: Nexus Architect
architect.desc: A proactive AI overlay that periodically generates innovative ideas and suggestions for improving NexusOS. Ideas appear as floating notifications on the desktop.
architect.tips: Approve ideas to let the system implement them, or dismiss to skip.

identity_shield.name: Nexus Identity Shield
identity_shield.desc: A holographic biometric security interface evolved from the legacy login system. Features animated scanning effects and multi-factor authentication simulation.
identity_shield.tips: Access from the Start Menu. The scanning animation is purely cosmetic but demonstrates the UI framework capabilities.

nexus_vault.name: Nexus Vault Backup
nexus_vault.desc: A smart backup management system. Schedule, monitor, and manage system backups with task queuing, live progress logs, and smart pause functionality.
nexus_vault.tips: Set up automated backup schedules to protect your important data.

neural_hub.name: Neural Hub
neural_hub.desc: A centralized dashboard for monitoring all AI services, agents, and daemon statuses. Provides a bird's-eye view of the entire NexusOS neural network.
neural_hub.tips: Check here to see which AI services are online and their current processing state.

omnishield.name: OmniShield Protection Service
omnishield.desc: The unified system protection layer running silently in the background. Combines the Zombie Port Sniper (kills processes blocking critical ports), NPM Auto-Installer (heals missing module errors), and Predictive Threat Analysis (uses AI to predict failures from log patterns).
omnishield.tips: OmniShield is always active. Check the System Monitor for the real-time Protection Ledger and Health Score.

lock_screen.name: OmniShield Gateway (Lock Screen)
lock_screen.desc: The secure entry point to NexusOS. Features a zero-trust authentication protocol with passcode entry and simulated biometric fingerprint scanning. Displays real-time clock and system status.
lock_screen.tips: Default credentials: Admin / nexus2026. Use the fingerprint button for quick biometric access.

context_menu.name: Desktop Context Menu
context_menu.desc: Right-click anywhere on the desktop for Windows-style options: View (icon sizes), Sort By, Refresh, New Folder, Open Terminal, Display Settings, Personalize, and Lock Terminal.
context_menu.tips: Use the View submenu to switch between Large, Medium, and Small desktop icons.

ghost_veil.name: Nexus Ghost Veil
ghost_veil.desc: A cutting-edge privacy innovation tool designed to mask human identities in photos. Uses simulated AI segmentation to apply neural blurs, holographic glitches, or silhouette voids to bodies, while scrubbing all EXIF metadata for absolute privacy.
ghost_veil.tips: Integrated directly with Pantheon Gallery. Select any photo and click 'Enter the Veil' to begin the identity masking process.
